name: Dolmens Trail
name_original: Giro ad anello da Baião a Dólmen das Meninas do Crasto
description: This moderate 20.8-mile mountain biking loop from Baião explores the Serra da Aboboreira, visiting ancient dolmens and scenic viewpoints.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Campelo offers diverse terrain, characterized by hilly landscapes and varied elevations suitable for different skill levels. The region provides a mix of challenging climbs and descents, often traversing through natural areas. Mountain bike trails in Campelo feature paths that wind through rural settings and offer views of the surrounding countryside. Best mountain bike trails around Campelo The most popular mountain bike trail is Dolmens Trail, a 20.8 miles (33.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This route features varied terrain with significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Baião - Serra do Marão Black Route, a difficult 40.6 miles (65.4 km) path. This trail offers extensive climbs and descents through mountainous landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Loop tour from Baião towards the Miradouro da Nossa Senhora da Guia, a 13.6 miles (22.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 16 minutes. Mountain biking around Campelo is defined by hilly terrain, forest paths, and river valleys. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels, ensuring challenges for experienced riders. The routes in Campelo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 5 reviews. More than 170 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Campelo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Campelo?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails around Campelo, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The region is known for its hilly terrain, forest paths, and river valleys.

Q: What is the general terrain like for mountain biking in Campelo?
A: Mountain biking around Campelo features diverse terrain, characterized by hilly landscapes and varied elevations. You'll encounter a mix of challenging climbs and descents, often traversing through natural areas and rural settings, with paths that wind through forests and river valleys.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Campelo?
A: While Campelo offers many challenging routes, there are 8 moderate trails available. These routes provide a good balance for riders looking for an engaging experience without extreme difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2321502946/dolmens-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmens Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option, covering 20.8 miles with significant elevation gain.

Q: What options are there for experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge?
A: Experienced mountain bikers will find plenty of challenges in Campelo, with 9 difficult trails available. These routes feature extensive climbs and descents through mountainous landscapes. A notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2321520376/baiao-serra-do-marao-black-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baião - Serra do Marão Black Route&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 40.6 miles and includes over 2,100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes for a full day out?
A: Yes, Campelo offers several long-distance routes perfect for a full day of riding. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32161549" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Salvador de Tabuado – Meninas do Crasto Dolmen loop from Marco de Canaveses&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 43.1-mile trail that can take over 7 hours to complete, offering extensive climbs and descents.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Campelo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Campelo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from 5 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the challenging climbs and descents, and the scenic views of the surrounding countryside.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Campelo?
A: Yes, Campelo features several circular mountain bike routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2321489526/loop-tour-from-baiao-towards-the-miradouro-da-nossa-senhora-da-guia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop tour from Baião towards the Miradouro da Nossa Senhora da Guia&lt;/a&gt;, a 13.6-mile trail that offers varied terrain and can be completed in about 2 hours and 16 minutes.

Q: What kind of scenic views can I expect on mountain bike trails in Campelo?
A: Many mountain bike trails in Campelo offer scenic views of the surrounding countryside, including hilly landscapes, forest paths, and river valleys. For panoramic vistas, consider routes that pass by viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4199698" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Nossa Senhora da Guia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or natural attractions to see along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, you can combine your ride with visits to historical and natural attractions. Some routes pass near ancient sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3790762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Meninas do Crasto Dolmen&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3790686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Chã da Parada&lt;/a&gt;. For natural beauty, look for trails that lead to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4460565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Pool and Waterfall Trail&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3678740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabrum Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that follow river paths or ecotrails?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate river paths and ecotrails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3568170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tâmega Line Ecotrail&lt;/a&gt; is a notable cycle way in the area, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4312974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azenhas Trail (Tâmega River, Amarante)&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic path along the Tâmega River.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike trails in Campelo?
A: Trail durations in Campelo vary significantly. You can find shorter routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2321489526/loop-tour-from-baiao-towards-the-miradouro-da-nossa-senhora-da-guia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop tour from Baião towards the Miradouro da Nossa Senhora da Guia&lt;/a&gt;, which takes around 2 hours and 16 minutes. Longer, more challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2321520376/baiao-serra-do-marao-black-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baião - Serra do Marão Black Route&lt;/a&gt; can take over 6 hours to complete.
tips: Another megalith in the Serra da Aboboreira. These places of worship are easily accessible from the road.
Great view over the Aboboreira mountains and the whole valley of the Serra do Castelo. The small chapel is used for pilgrimages in August

Baião is famous for its green and hilly landscapes that extend to the Douro River. It is located in a predominantly rural region, known for the production of Vinho Verde and citrus fruits. It offers an authentic experience of traditional Portugal, with a rich natural and cultural heritage and exquisite local gastronomy.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the mountain biking route from Baião to Dólmen das Meninas do Crasto?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route is generally considered difficult. It requires good preparation and technical skills due to diverse obstacles, drops up to 0.5m, and challenging climbs with maximum grades exceeding 15%. The terrain is highly variable, often consisting of granite, and is best suited for experienced and fit mountain bikers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking loop?
faqs.answer: You can expect highly variable terrain, characteristic of the Serra da Aboboreira. The route features granite surfaces, technical sections, and challenging climbs. Riders should be prepared for obstacles, drops, and unpredictable trail surfaces, necessitating a quality mountain bike.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Loop course from Baião to Dólmen das Meninas do Crasto?
faqs.answer: While specific parking for this exact loop isn't detailed, the Baião Mountain Bike Centre often serves as a starting and ending point for various circular routes in the area and provides support infrastructure. It's a good place to start your ride and inquire about local parking options.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route passes by several significant points of interest. You'll encounter the ancient Neolithic funerary monument, Meninas do Crasto Dolmen, and enjoy panoramic views from the Viewpoint of Nossa Senhora da Guia. The trail also offers glimpses into rural agricultural practices and other megalithic monuments in the Serra da Aboboreira.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this mountain biking trail is not recommended for beginners or families. It is classified as difficult, featuring technical sections, significant climbs, and obstacles that require advanced skills and physical fitness. More experienced riders will find it challenging and rewarding.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain biking loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this loop is typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) months. During these periods, the weather is generally milder and more stable, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the potentially wet and cold conditions of winter, which can make the technical terrain even more challenging.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the mountain biking trails in the Baião region?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in the Baião region, but it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and agricultural areas. Be mindful of other trail users and ensure your dog is well-behaved and can handle the challenging terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for mountain biking in Serra da Aboboreira?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on the trails within the Serra da Aboboreira, including this loop. However, always respect local regulations, private property, and environmental guidelines while riding.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other mountain biking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other trails in the region. Notably, it covers sections of the "Trilho dos Dólmens" (21% of the route), which is dedicated to exploring megalithic structures. It also briefly touches upon the "Trilho das Florestas Naturais" (8%) and the "Camino Torres - 02 Caminho de Don Diego de Torres Villarroel" (6%), among others.

faqs.question: What amenities are available near the trailhead in Baião?
faqs.answer: The town of Baião, which serves as the starting point, offers various amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation options. The Baião Mountain Bike Centre also provides support infrastructure for riders.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this challenging loop?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging nature of this loop, you should bring a well-maintained, quality mountain bike, appropriate safety gear including a helmet, sufficient water and snacks, a repair kit, and navigation tools. Technical riding skills and good physical fitness are also essential.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint of Nossa Senhora da Guia
